creator: asomers@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Ivan Rozhuk from comment #10)
Always sending mtime along with atime would be exactly the wrong thing for the kernel to do.  Only sending atime is what it's supposed to do.

Looking at the code you sent, sshfs_utimens is ignoring the tv_nsec field, which is clearly a bug.  If those fields are set to UTIME_OMIT then it shouldn't set that timestamp.  This is a bug in sshfs, and you should report it upstream.

Did your friend use "-o strictatime"?  It looks like that mount option is required on Linux in order for it to actually update the atime.
